Package component.mover

Class Delay

java.lang.Object
component.mover.Mover
component.mover.Delay
All Implemented Interfaces:
Tippable, GridIndexable
public class Delay extends Mover
The Delay is a stateful Mover that stalls card movement.

A card entering this tile will be forced to remain for one additional logic tick before the mover returns its actual rotation direction. This is essential for timing synchronization in complex card pathways.

  • Constructor Summary

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • Delay

      public Delay(Direction rot)
      Constructs a Delay mover with a specified exit direction.
      Parameters:
      rot - The Direction the card will eventually move toward.

  • Method Details

    • getDirectionStateless

      public Direction getDirectionStateless()
      Determines the movement direction without updating the internal state.

      If the card currently on the tile matches the card stored during the last getDirection() call, the delay is considered complete.

      Specified by:
      getDirectionStateless in class Mover
      Returns:
      The Direction of rotation if the delay is over; otherwise Direction.STAY.

    • getDirection

      public Direction getDirection()
      Updates the delay state and returns the current allowed direction.

      This method is called by the game engine during the movement phase. It returns the result of the delay check and then caches the current card to prepare for the next tick.

      Overrides:
      getDirection in class Mover
      Returns:
      The movement Direction for the current tick.

    • getValidOutputDirections

      public Direction[] getValidOutputDirections()
      Defines the valid exit for this mover.
      Specified by:
      getValidOutputDirections in class Mover
      Returns:
      A single-element array containing the Direction of rotation.

    • getTooltip

      public Tooltip getTooltip()
      Generates a tooltip explaining the 2-tick transit time.
      Specified by:
      getTooltip in interface Tippable
      Overrides:
      getTooltip in class Mover
      Returns:
      A Tooltip detailing the delay mechanics.
